titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Janet Malcolm Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 12254809.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday June 17, 2021 at 03:00

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of research materials, writings, correspondence, vertical files, printed material, audiovisual materials, and electronic media documenting the work and writings of Janet Malcolm. Research materials and writings consist of correspondence, legal documents, transcripts, notes, manuscripts, drafts, proofs, printed material, and other papers created or collected by Janet Malcolm in the course of researching or writing various projects including Psychoanalysis: The Impossible Profession (Alfred A. Knopf, 1981), In the Freud Archives (Alfred A. Knopf, 1984), The Journalist and the Murderer (Alfred A. Knopf, 1990), The Silent Woman: Sylvia Plath and Ted Hughes (Alfred A. Knopf, 1994), The Crime of Sheila McGough (Alfred A. Knopf, 1999), and others. Research materials also include files of correspondence and related papers from J. D. Salinger and Phillip Roth. Vertical files consist of additional writings, correspondence, notebooks, and research materials. Audiovisual materials consist of audiocassettes containing interviews and other recordings generated by Malcolm while conducting research.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Boxes 55-58, 85-95 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 59, 86 (digital media): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 101: Restricted until 2103. For further information consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into six series: I. Research materials and writings, 1950-2010. II. Vertical files, 1940-2009. III. Printed materials, 1964-2007. IV. Audiovisual materials, circa 1970s-2005. V. Electronic media, circa 1990s. VI. February 2020 acquisition, 1933-2018

bioghist.head: Janet Malcolm
bioghist.p: Janet Malcolm (born 1934) is an American writer and journalist. A longtime staff writer for The New Yorker and author of numerous books, Malcolm has written on a range of subjects including photography, psychoanalysis, Sylvia Plath and Ted Hughes, and Gertrude Stein and Alice B. Toklas.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Janet Malcolm on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2013.
February 2020 acquisition: Purchased from Janet Malcolm on the American Literature Fund and the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2020.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Janet Malcom Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Janet Malcolm Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Research materials and writings are arranged roughly by project.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Research materials and writings consist correspondence, legal documents, transcripts, notes, manuscripts, drafts, proofs, printed material, and other papers created or collected by Janet Malcolm in the course of researching or writing various projects. They document the following works: Psychoanalysis: The Impossible Profession (Alfred A. Knopf, 1981), In the Freud Archives (Alfred A. Knopf, 1984), The Journalist and the Murderer (Alfred A. Knopf, 1990), The Silent Woman: Sylvia Plath and Ted Hughes (Alfred A. Knopf, 1994), The Crime of Sheila McGough (Alfred A. Knopf, 1999), Reading Chekhov: A Critical Journey (Random House, 2001), Two Lives: Gertrude and Alice (Yale University Press, 2007), and Iphigenia in Forest Hills: Anatomy of a Murder Trial (Yale University Press, 2011). Also includes research files on the Supreme Court, Ingri Sischy, and other topics, as well as files of correspondence and related papers from J. D. Salinger and Phillip Roth.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: For related materials, see also Series II. Vertical files.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Janet Malcolm's vertical files were kept in two filing units with three and four drawers, respectively. The arrangement of the files maintains their order by file and drawer as organized by Malcolm.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vertical files consists of incoming and outgoing correspondence, writings, writings by others, notebooks, and other papers relating to Janet Malcolm's research and writings.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: For related materials, see also Series I. Research materials and writings.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Janet Malcolm on the American Literature Fund and the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2020.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four subseries: Research materials and writings, 1933-2018; Audiovisual materials, 1975-2012, undated; Computer media, 2009-2017, undated; and Restricted papers, circa 2007-2010.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 101: Restricted until 2103. For further information consult the appropriate curator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These folders contain research material for multiple projects including files related to Forty-one False Starts , notebooks, correspondence, expense reports, and other related documentation. Also includes interviews with individuals including Bill Johnson, Brooks Adams, David Salle, Dodie Kazonjion, Karole Armitage, Mary Boone, and Anne Goldstein.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These folders contain research material for multiple projects including files related to David Salle, Roberta Smith, plastic surgery, women in Brooklyn, and notes and interviews.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These folders contain research material for multiple projects including files related to David Salle, Making it, Nobody's looking at you, Ian Buruma, Sarah French, and Podhoretz, as well as calendars.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These folders contain research material for multiple projects including files related to Podhoretz galleys, Making it, Two lives, reviews, and translation piece.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These folders contain research material for multiple projects including files related to Forty one false starts, Nobody's looking at you, and printed material and calendar pages.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These folders contain research material for multiple projects including files related to Rachel Maddow, Yuja Wang, Rolling Stone, and tax show.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These folders contain research material for multiple projects including files related to Rachel Maddow and Yuja Wang.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These folders contain research material for multiple projects including files related to Eileen Fisher and Ted Hughes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These folders contain research material for multiple projects including files related to Life of a book shop, Ted Hughes, Eileen Fisher, Gertrude Stein, Forty one false starts and printed material, calendars, and notebook.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These folders contain research material for multiple projects including files related to Eileen Fisher, Life in a book shop, Dressed to play, Rachel Maddow, and Storyteller, and calendars and printed material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These folders contain research material for multiple projects including files related to Forty one false starts, Gertrude Stein, and Title TK, and calendars.

did.unittitle: Research materials related to Iphigenia in Forest Hills: Anatomy of a Murder Trial
Research materials related to Iphigenia in Forest Hills: Anatomy of a Murder Trial
Research materials related to Iphigenia in Forest Hills: Anatomy of a Murder Trial
Research materials related to Iphigenia in Forest Hills: Anatomy of a Murder Trial

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Books edited by Edward Burns of Gertrude Stein letters.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Books written by Ulla E. Dydo, Donald Gallup, and Janet Hubhouse.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Books by Linda Sime, Getrude Stein, and Virgil Thomson.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Books by Alice B. Toklas and Leo Tolstoy.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Books by Paul Alexander, Norman Podhoretz, and Leo Tolstoy.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Books by Paul Alexander, Thomas Bredsdorf, Lynda K. Bundtzen, Edward Butscher, Rose Leiman Goldemberg, and Ronald Hayman.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Books by Lucas Myer, Charles Newman, and Sylvia Plath.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Books by Sylvia Plath and Anne Stevenson.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Books by Ronald Hingley, Saverly Senderovich, Jule de Sherbinin, and Andrei Turkov.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Books by Peter Davison, Rachel Maddow, Norman Podheretz, Jacqueline Rose, Penolope Shuttle, and Peter Redgrove.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: E. Fisher 1st interview, Eileen Fisher 2012 2 Jan.
April 8 A, David Salle April 8 B
Side A. Naomi First Interview, Side B
A Ben Janet beginning A, B Janet on A-over BEN Empty
Recordings related to Psychoanalysis: The Impossible Profession
Leon B I I Beautiful Woman 1/2 WB Done, Len B II DONE
Leon 22 he gets impatient re[?] DONE 2, Leon 22 "The work is unhealthy" [?] DONE 2
Leon 14 acting/lying Koch seeing things under the micro-scope DONE "looking for ?" [?], Leon 14 DONE
Leon Balter 5 end of morning. About my writing, Frances E 2 hour
Leon Baler 7 Orwell detachment O[?] desivelessness of analyst. DPME
Leon 19 I say his case isn't "modern" but like old Vienna we start to talk about Ego-psych mention of Hurting Dahl DONE, Leon 19 primary & secondary processes DONE
False Leon 25 patients have "changed" "good" & "bad" people patients in advertising Part I, Leon 25
Balter 3 Feb 20, 1979 Done, Balter 3 Bettleheim Done
Leon 20 me [?] case DONE, Leon 20 I say I feel frustrated because he doesn't let me talk about myself
Balter 4 no nature in psychoanalyst child's wordl the paradijm Kohut DONE, Curricht-story Done
Leon 22 Galenh[?] discussion of analyst & "real [?] good DONE, Leon 22 gossip DONE
Leon B 12 selection of psyh[?]- cs Strategic DONE, Leon B 12 First Case [?] again DONE
[?] Leon 18 women he hates Me reading from journal DONE, empty

1 7 Psychoanalyst Soc. [?] Jan 9, 77.0Present: [?] N. Y. Psychoanalytic Institute Inner Sanctum, Other Side First Leon Balter 7th session inner sancturm his brother-in-law ing meeting with Elders Good
Balder 8 done on Brena & Arl[?], Susan Barlow Apr. 75
Spiegal Balder 9 Done (1) Anna Freud-learning from Failure., Done Balder 9 being sucked into Institute-like fall in love
Leon Balter 6 (1) discussion of F Epstein, empty
Leon 15 doing different things DONE, empty
Balder 5 Bremer's joy stick-& passage on not saying you're sorry. Done, Balder 5 H. D.'s outburst love in [?]
Leon 21 Kohut Mr Z done, Leon 21
Leon Balter 1 Done, Leon Balter Surgery analogy done
David & Lisa His Dog/Bremers (Over) Table DONE, DONE 30 min
Eric Jong Balter 9 (3) Psychotic Episodes in analysis, Beres Arthur group Balder 9 DONE (?)
Leon 19, A2 19 Ar[?] & Bremmer;s bok Kohut like a God separated me from the masses DONE
Leon Balter 24 M[?] Geo K | case history. DONE Sept 18, On tape-recording First patient [?] L Balder supervision as as Dream Aul[?] Wangh on tape recording
Leon 14 Bremer's [?]oticval is m layers of me [?] Freud, Jong nomoset [?], Leon 14
2 Balter 8 Ambition (2) baseball analogy, Balter 9 Chicago corporations Done 2 end
1 M[?] Leon Balter 11 annoying neighbor Freud as artist done, Balter 11 * Side 2 Charlie Cuskin [?]
Leon 26 Frances E 1 See end of tape, [?] Leon 26 done
I Leon 11 Done psychic determination & dete[?], I Leon Balter 11 DONE
Beauty of the [?] position DONE Leon Balter 8 No Son, DONE Leon Balter R & R again His acceptance at SF & NY.
Leon Balter 13 DONE [?] to bring [?], [?] DONE
Side 1 Discussion of case. I read [?] n. b. 15, Leon 15
Leon 16 discussion of regression, Leon 16 notebook of other case
Leon Balter (2) Woman analyst telling who your [?] is. DONE, DONE Leon Balter session at N.Y. Psychoanalystic
DONE Leon Balter 10 on [?]-not being [?], Leon 10 about my portrait of him "all my words" DONE
Hiller Swiller Leon 28 his patients-3 sessions going badly done, 1 the woman who was false Leon 28 Dan Schartz's position done
Done Leon/May 12, transference, love beginnings Done 2 (Balter)
[?] worry that I'll be found out & condemmed"-near end. DONE Leon 16 Swas a nonentity [?] sessions of 1 case, I feel sad on hurting Dahl DONE
Leon Balter 6 DONE Scandal, Psy[?] as science his parents & English 6

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted until 2103. For further information consult the appropriate curator.
